protein_name: Guanine nucleotide-binding proteins (G proteins) are involved as modulators or transducers in various transmembrane signaling systems. The G(i) proteins are involved in hormonal regulation of adenylate cyclase: they inhibit the cyclase in response to beta-adrenergic stimuli. May play a role in cell division. Isoform sGi2: Regulates the cell surface density of dopamine receptors DRD2 by sequestrating them as an intracellular pool. Belongs to the G-alpha family. G(i/o/t/z) subfamily. 6 alternatively spliced human isoforms have been reported. Note: This description may include information from UniProtKB.
